title: How mRNA Vaccines Teach Your Cells
script_summary: This script explains how mRNA vaccines work without altering your DNA, focusing on the delivery mechanism and immune response.

segments.voiceover: mRNA vaccines don’t rewrite your DNA — they deliver temporary instructions.
Inside your body, tiny lipid nanoparticles act like delivery trucks, bringing mRNA straight to your cells.
These mRNA strands provide instructions to make a harmless piece of the virus — just enough to train your immune system.
Research shows these instructions never integrate into your DNA, ensuring your genetic code remains untouched.
Instead, they teach your immune system to recognize the virus, preparing it for a real encounter.
Curious about how else vaccines work? Hit that follow button for more science insights!
